finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:135 — Dan's first substantive line now reads '"I mean —" He stopped. "They froze your accounts."' The hedging qualifier 'I mean' arrives reflexively (one of his listed syntax-signature markers) and is cut short by the gravity of the fact, marking the shift from his baseline qualifying register to pressure-driven directness. chapters/chapter_14.md:165 — After acknowledging 'Yours was worse,' Dan's thumb snags the armrest fabric and he says '"The upholstery's going."' This is a concrete physical observation (texture, a domestic surface detail) that reveals his specific perceptual habit under stress: fixating on irrelevant tactile details when the abstract becomes unbearable, per his style-bible stress_tells. chapters/chapter_14.md:159 — The pre-existing stress-tell repetition ('Nothing. Nothing happened. That's what happened.') remains unchanged and continues to function as a third distinctive Dan marker.
finding_results.notes: Two new Dan-specific voice markers added to supplement the existing marker at line 159, giving the couch scene three lines with distinctive Dan idiolect. Edit 1 (line 135) deploys a hedging false start ('I mean —') that gets abandoned — showing Dan's qualifier-first syntax interrupted by emotional weight, audibly marking his shift to the direct register the style bible specifies for genuine upset. Edit 2 (line 165) deploys a concrete physical observation ('The upholstery's going') immediately after an emotional admission, enacting his stress tell of grabbing onto textures and surfaces when the larger situation exceeds his language. Both edits are minimal (no surrounding narration changed) to avoid collateral churn. No continuity details introduced or contradicted; the armrest is part of the established couch in the living room. Contraction levels verified: Dan's 'high' contraction level is honored throughout, and Mira's 'moderate' level is maintained. No example_lines from the style bible appear in the chapter.

summary: Single finding F14-09 (MEDIUM) addressed with two surgical dialogue edits. Dan's first substantive line in the couch scene now opens with a hedging false start ('I mean —') that is abandoned under pressure, deploying his syntax-signature qualifying habit. His line at the scene's emotional midpoint now includes a concrete physical observation about the couch fabric, enacting his stress tell of fixating on irrelevant tactile details. Combined with the pre-existing stress-tell repetition at line 159, three of Dan's eleven lines now carry distinctive idiolect markers. The scene's rhythm, Mira's POV narration, leverage shifts, and contraction registers are preserved. No continuity changes, no heading-contract violations, no sanitization.
